items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we have the Swiss Q1 GDP, the Eurozone unemployment rate and the final PMIs for the major Eurozone economies and the UK. None of the data should change anything for the respective central banks at this point. As a reminder, the SNB isn't expected to adjust interest rates anytime soon. The SNB has vowed to intervene in the FX market when necessary but haven't done much. The ECB is widely expected to hike in June and then take a pause at least until September to see how the data and the US-Iran situation evolves over the summer. Lastly, the BoE is leaning more towards holding rates steady for longer rather than opting for rate hikes. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the main highlight is the US ISM Manufacturing PMI which is expected to tick higher to 53.0 vs 52.7 in the prior month. The S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI showed that factory growth was again in part supported by temporary stock building and order book growth had been somewhat subdued by the ongoing war in the Middle East, most notably in terms of export sales. Moreover, input costs jumped in May at the steepest rate since late-2022 on the back of rising war-related supply constraints and steep energy cost increases. Costs were not only cited as causing lower sales but also contributed to steepening job losses and a further rise in selling price inflation to its highest since August 2022. The next NFP and CPI reports will be more important for the Fed ahead of the 16-17 June FOMC meeting. If we get upside surprises and the situation in the Strait of Hormuz doesn't change before the meeting, we might get a hawkish surprise. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 07:30 GMT/03:30 ET - ECB's Schnabel (hawkish - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，我们将迎来瑞士第一季度GDP、欧元区失业率，以及欧元区主要经济体和英国的最终PMI数据。目前来看，这些数据都不太可能改变各央行现有的政策走向。需要提醒的是，预计瑞士央行短期内不会调整利率。瑞士央行曾承诺在必要时干预外汇市场，但迄今行动有限。市场普遍预期欧洲央行将在6月加息，随后至少暂停至9月，以观察夏季期间经济数据及美伊局势的发展。最后，英国央行更倾向于维持利率不变更长时间，而非选择加息。美股时段 美股时段的主要焦点是美国ISM制造业PMI，预计将小幅升至53.0，高于上月的52.7。标普全球美国制造业PMI数据显示，工厂增长再次部分得益于临时性库存积压，而订单增长则因中东持续战事而有所受抑，尤其体现在出口销售方面。 此外，受战争引发的供应瓶颈加剧以及能源成本急剧攀升的影响，5月份投入成本的涨幅创下2022年底以来的最高纪录。成本问题不仅被视为导致销售额下降的原因，还加剧了失业率的上升，并推动销售价格通胀进一步攀升至2022年8月以来的最高水平。在6月16日至17日联邦公开市场委员会（FOMC）会议召开前，接下来的非农就业报告（NFP）和消费者物价指数（CPI）报告对美联储而言将更为重要。若数据出现超预期上行，且霍尔木兹海峡局势在会议前未发生变化，我们可能会迎来鹰派意外。央行官员讲话 格林尼治时间07:30/美国东部时间03:30 - 欧洲央行施纳贝尔（鹰派 - 投票委员） 本文由investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔撰写。
items.summaryEn: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we have the Swiss Q1 GDP, the Eurozone unemployment rate and the final PMIs for the major Eurozone economies and the UK. None of the data should change anything for the respective central banks at this point. As a reminder, the SNB isn't expected to adjust interest rates anytime soon. The SNB has vowed to intervene in the FX market when necessary but haven't done much. The ECB is widely expected to hike in June and then take a pause at least until September to see how the data and the US-Iran situation evolves over the summer. Lastly, the BoE is leaning more towards holding rates steady for longer rather than opting for rate hikes. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the main highlight is the US ISM Manufacturing PMI which is expected to tick higher to 53.0 vs 52.7 in the prior month. The S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI showed that factory growth was again in part supported by temporary stock building and order book growth had been somewhat subdued by the ongoing war in the Middle East, most notably in terms of export sales. Moreover, input costs jumped in May at the steepest rate since late-2022 on the back of rising war-related supply constraints and steep energy cost increases. Costs were not only cited as causing lower sales but also contributed to steepening job losses and a further rise in selling price inflation to its highest since August 2022. The next NFP and CPI reports will be more important for the Fed ahead of the 16-17 June FOMC meeting. If we get upside surprises and the situation in the Strait of Hormuz doesn't change before the meeting, we might get a hawkish surprise. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 07:30 GMT/03:30 ET - ECB's Schnabel (hawkish - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Oil prices up. Deal elusive, attacks continue.
items.summary: Reports of another likely ballistic missile interception near Ali Al Salem Airbase, Kuwait Japan Q1 capex growth stalls at near zero, raising risk of GDP revision lower ECB's Schnabel says Iran war inflation too broad to look through, flags rate hikes China tightens outbound investment rules to curb technology and data transfers Rubio pushes Lebanon ceasefire but US says Hezbollah blocked deal on Iran's orders Is Palantir a Buy? China May private survey Rating Dog Manufacturing PMI 51.8 (expected 51.4, prior 52.2) Reports of Iran drafting its own deal amendments as Tehran pushes back on Trump's terms PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8167 (vs. estimate at 6.7643Z) Nasdaq technical analysis today at investingLive.com Japan manufacturing PMI eases to 54.5 in May. Cost pressures hit 32-month high Powell warns Fed independence at risk if officials can be removed over policy Bitcoin Futures Analysis Today: BTC Stalls Below 74,535 Japan Capital Spending Q1: 0.0% y/y (expected 4.0%, prior 6.5%) UK businesses remain deeply downbeat on outlook despite slight mood lift in May Australia manufacturing PMI slips to 50.7 in May as new orders hit seven-month low Iran is calling the resignation report fake news. Iran's president resigns, says IRGC seized control and cut him out of decisions US Globex open for Sunday evening trade and the new week. Oil up, Stocks little change. Berkshire Hathaway to buy Taylor Morrison in $8.5bn all-cash housing deal US military guiding ships through Strait of Hormuz. NY Times says 70 in last 3 weeks. US Commerce Department moves to block Nvidia and AMD chip flows to Chinese overseas units Iran says no nuclear commitments made as talks with US continue China manufacturing PMI falls to 50 in May as export orders contract Monday open FX. Indicative rates 01 June 2026 Should you sell DELL stock or move your stop higher? Practical lesson in trade management Week in Focus: 1st - 5th June: Highlights include US ISMs, NFP, EZ HICP & CAD jobs US Bessent on Iran blockade says anything that is taken off will be taken off slowly At a glance: Oil opens higher as US-Iran deal remains elusive; Trump says he is in no hurry, warns military action could resume if talks collapse Iran's President Pezeshkian reportedly asked to resign, citing IRGC takeover of decision-making; Iranian state media and government deny the report Rubio spoke with Lebanese President Aoun and Israeli PM Netanyahu on ceasefire initiative; US official warns failure could give Israel green light to resume Beirut strikes Missile and drone attacks reported near Ali Al Salem Airbase in Kuwait; Kuwaiti air defences engaged US Navy quietly escorted around 70 vessels through Hormuz over three weeks, averaging three per day against a pre-war baseline of 100-plus daily China RatingDog/S&P Global Manufacturing PMI eases to 51.8 in May from 52.2; input inflation slows for first time in six months but remains above long-run average ECB's Schnabel signals rate hikes ahead, says Iran war inflation too broad to look through Fed's Kashkari warns supply chain and inflation recovery could lag even after Hormuz reopens; BOJ's Koeda flags oil as negative supply shock for Japan US closes AI chip export loophole; hundreds of thousands of advanced Nvidia and AMD chips may have reached Chinese firms via overseas subsidiaries over the past year Berkshire Hathaway agrees to acquire Taylor Morrison for $8.5bn, a 24% premium to Friday's close USD edges higher; regional equities extend record run; US futures open soft before turning positive Oil prices climbed at the open and regional sentiment stayed cautious as the Iran-US nuclear framework showed fresh signs of strain, a reported presidential resignation in Tehran rattled confidence in the diplomatic track, and Washington moved to close a year-long loophole that had been quietly feeding China's AI ambitions. Iran: the deal and the denial The session's dominant story was a report, first published by Iran International, that President Masoud Pezeshkian had submitted a resignation to the Supreme Leader's office, citing his exclusion from major decisions and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' effective takeover of government. According to the report, Pezeshkian told Khamenei directly that he and his administration had been sidelined, leaving him unable to fulfil his responsibilities as president. Iran's Government Information Council pushed back hard, describing the story as foreign propaganda designed to sow discord and insisting Pezeshkian was actively running the country. Iranian state media also denied the report. The denial did not land cleanly. The specific detail in the original account, that the IRGC had assumed control of all major decision-making and that the elected presidency had been rendered ceremonial, was too precise and too politically consequential to be dismissed as routine disinformation, and markets treated it accordingly. The timing is acutely damaging for the negotiations. Washington has been engaging with Iran's civilian foreign ministry on a framework that would extend the ceasefire, begin reopening the Strait of Hormuz and launch structured nuclear talks. If Pezeshkian's account is accurate, the body the US has been negotiating with has been operating without a mandate from the institution that controls Iran's missiles, its naval forces in the strait, and its regional proxies. That is not a technicality. It is a fundamental question about whether any agreement reached with Iranian civilian officials can be implemented or honoured. Trump, speaking to Fox News on Saturday, said he was in no hurry to reach a deal, adding that a rushed negotiation would produce a bad outcome. He stressed he was pressing for a framework that would permanently prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon and warned that if talks collapsed, military action could resume. Separately, a US official told Axios that the failure of Secretary Rubio's Lebanon ceasefire push, which sought a Hezbollah halt to attacks as a first step toward de-escalation, could lead Washington to give Israel a green light to resume strikes on Beirut targets. Reports of missile and drone attacks near Ali Al Salem Airbase in Kuwait added to the session's geopolitical noise. Shipping: a trickle, not a reopening The New York Times reported that US Central Command had quietly coordinated passage for around 70 commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz over three weeks, with ships running dark to avoid Iranian detection. The figure is higher than some analysts had expected but needs context: before the Trump war began, more than 100 vessels transited the strait daily. Three coordinated crossings per day is a managed trickle, not a restoration of normal traffic, and the covert nature of the operation means independent verification is impossible. Central banks ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel struck a clearly hawkish note, saying the bank can no longer look through the Iran war's inflationary impact now that price pressures have spread beyond energy and the risk of unanchored expectations has risen. She said infrastructure damage and supply chain disruption have altered price dynamics in a lasting way and declined to put a ceiling on the number of rate hikes required, saying the ECB would continue assessing data and regional developments. Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari offered a sobering counterpoint on the relief-rally thesis. Even if the Strait of Hormuz were to reopen tomorrow, he said, the speed at which supply chains and inflation recover could be considerably slower than markets might expect. It was a pointed warning against pricing in a rapid normalisation of energy and goods costs simply on the back of a ceasefire announcement. US monetary policy remains somewhat restrictive, he noted, and the Hormuz reopening is the key variable determining the path forward. Bank of Japan Policy Board member Junko Koeda noted that oil represents a negative supply shock for Japan, consistent with the BOJ's internal framing of the conflict's economic impact. Technology and markets The Commerce Department closed a loophole on Sunday that had for nearly a year allowed Chinese firms to acquire Nvidia Blackwell, Rubin and AMD MI350x chips through overseas subsidiaries, primarily in locations such as Malaysia, without a licence. One industry source estimated that hundreds of thousands of chips may have been exported through the gap, potentially undermining one of Washington's central tools for slowing China's AI development. The guidance does not require existing installations to be shut down. Berkshire Hathaway agreed to acquire homebuilder Taylor Morrison for $8.5 billion in cash, at a 24% premium to Friday's close, in a deal expected to close in the second half of the year. The US dollar edged higher on war risk. Regional equities extended their record run. US equity index futures opened soft before turning positive. Trump's excursion is now a war in its fourth month. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 有报道称科威特阿里·萨勒姆空军基地附近可能再次发生弹道导弹拦截事件 日本第一季度资本支出增长停滞于接近零的水平，加剧了GDP下修的风险 欧洲央行施纳贝尔表示，伊朗战争引发的通胀影响过于广泛，无法忽视，并暗示将加息 中国收紧对外投资规则以遏制技术和数据转移 卢比奥推动黎巴嫩停火，但美国称真主党奉伊朗之命阻挠了协议 帕兰蒂尔（Palantir）值得买入吗？中国5月私营部门调查：制造业PMI为51.8（预期51.4，前值52.2） 有报道称伊朗正在起草自己的协议修正案，因德黑兰方面拒绝接受特朗普的条件。中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8167（市场预期为6.7643Z）。今日纳斯达克技术分析详见investingLive.com。日本5月制造业PMI降至54.5。 成本压力升至32个月高点 鲍威尔警告称，若因政策原因可罢免官员，美联储独立性将面临风险 今日比特币期货分析： 比特币在74,535美元下方停滞不前 日本第一季度资本支出：同比0.0%（预期4.0%，前值6.5%） 尽管5月情绪略有好转，英国企业对前景仍持深度悲观态度 澳大利亚5月制造业PMI降至50.7，新订单创七个月新低 伊朗称辞职报道为假新闻。伊朗总统辞职，称革命卫队夺取控制权并将其排除在决策之外。美国Globex交易所于周日晚间开盘，开启新一周交易。油价上涨，股市基本持平。伯克希尔·哈撒韦将以85亿美元全现金交易收购泰勒·莫里森。美军正引导舰船通过霍尔木兹海峡。《纽约时报》称过去三周已有70艘船只通过。美国商务部采取措施阻止英伟达和AMD向中国海外子公司供应芯片。伊朗表示，在与美国的谈判继续进行期间，未作出任何核承诺。中国5月制造业PMI降至50，出口订单萎缩。周一外汇市场开盘。 2026年6月1日参考汇率：是否该卖出戴尔股票，还是将止损位上移？交易管理实战课 本周焦点：6月1日至5日：重点包括美国ISM指数、非农就业数据、欧元区HICP及加拿大就业数据 美国贝森特谈伊朗封锁：任何解除的措施都将缓慢进行 简讯：美伊协议仍遥遥无期，油价高开； 特朗普表示不急于求成，警告若谈判破裂可能重启军事行动 据报道，伊朗总统佩泽什基安因革命卫队接管决策权而被要求辞职；伊朗官方媒体和政府否认该报道 卢比奥就停火倡议与黎巴嫩总统奥恩及以色列总理内塔尼亚胡进行交谈；美国官员警告，若停火失败可能给以色列重启贝鲁特空袭开绿灯 科威特阿里·萨勒姆空军基地附近据报发生导弹和无人机袭击； 科威特防空系统已启动 过去三周，美国海军悄然护送约70艘船只通过霍尔木兹海峡，日均护航量为3艘，而战前日均护航量超过100艘 中国RatingDog/标普全球制造业PMI5月从52.2降至51.8；投入通胀六个月来首次放缓，但仍高于长期平均水平 欧洲央行施纳贝尔暗示将加息 广告称，伊朗战争引发的通胀范围过于广泛，无法被忽视；美联储的卡什卡里警告称，即使霍尔木兹海峡重新开放，供应链和通胀的复苏也可能滞后； 日本央行委员小田指出油价对日本构成负面供给冲击 美国堵住AI芯片出口漏洞；过去一年中，数十万颗英伟达和AMD高端芯片可能已通过海外子公司流入中国企业 伯克希尔·哈撒韦同意以85亿美元收购泰勒·莫里森，较上周五收盘价溢价24% 美元小幅走高；区域股市延续创纪录涨势； 美股期货低开后转涨；油价开盘走高，但亚欧股市情绪仍显谨慎。因伊朗与美国核协议框架显现新的紧张迹象，德黑兰传出总统辞职的消息动摇了市场对外交谈判的信心，且华盛顿采取行动堵住了一个持续一年、曾悄然助长中国人工智能雄心的出口漏洞。 伊朗：协议与否认 本次会议的主要焦点是一则由“伊朗国际”频道率先发布的报道，称总统马苏德·佩泽什基安已向最高领袖办公室提交辞呈，理由是他被排除在重大决策之外，且伊斯兰革命卫队实际上已接管了政府。据报道，佩泽什基安直接向哈梅内伊表示，他及其政府已被边缘化，导致他无法履行总统职责。伊朗政府信息委员会对此予以强烈反驳，称该报道是旨在制造分裂的外国宣传，并坚称佩泽什基安正在积极治理国家。伊朗官方媒体也否认了该报道。这一否认未能完全平息事态。最初报道中的具体细节——即伊斯兰革命卫队已掌控所有重大决策，民选总统职位已沦为虚位——过于精准且政治影响深远，无法被简单归类为常规虚假信息，市场也据此作出了反应。这一消息的传出时机对谈判造成了严重损害。 华盛顿一直与伊朗民用外交部门就一项框架协议进行磋商，该协议旨在延长停火期限、开始重新开放霍尔木兹海峡，并启动有条不紊的核谈判。如果佩泽什基安的说法属实，那么美国一直与其谈判的机构，是在未获得伊朗导弹部队、海峡海军部队及其地区代理势力所隶属机构授权的情况下开展工作的。这绝非技术性问题。这关乎一个根本性问题：与伊朗文职官员达成的任何协议能否得到落实或遵守。特朗普周六接受福克斯新闻采访时表示，他并不急于达成协议，并补充说仓促的谈判将导致糟糕的结果。他强调，自己正在推动制定一项能够永久阻止伊朗获得核武器的框架，并警告称，如果谈判破裂，军事行动可能会重启。此外，一名美国官员向Axios透露，鲁比奥国务卿推动的黎巴嫩停火努力未能成功——该努力旨在让真主党停止袭击作为缓和局势的第一步——这可能导致华盛顿批准以色列重启对贝鲁特目标的打击。 关于科威特阿里·萨勒姆空军基地附近发生导弹和无人机袭击的报道，加剧了本轮会议的地缘政治动荡。航运：涓涓细流，而非全面重启 《纽约时报》报道称，过去三周内，美国中央司令部曾悄悄协调约70艘商船通过霍尔木兹海峡，这些船只均关闭航行灯以躲避伊朗侦测。这一数字虽高于部分分析师预期，但需结合背景来看：在特朗普政府挑起冲突之前，每天有超过100艘船只通过该海峡。每天三艘船的协调通行只是受控的涓流，而非正常交通的恢复，且行动的隐蔽性意味着无法进行独立核实。央行方面，欧洲央行执行委员会成员伊莎贝尔·施纳贝尔（Isabel Schnabel）明确释放了鹰派信号，称鉴于价格压力已蔓延至能源领域之外，且预期失控的风险上升，央行现已无法再忽视伊朗战争带来的通胀影响。 她表示，基础设施损毁和供应链中断已从根本上改变了价格走势，并拒绝为所需的加息次数设定上限，称欧洲央行将继续评估数据和地区局势。明尼阿波利斯联储主席尼尔·卡什卡里对“利好反弹”的说法提出了令人警醒的反驳。他表示，即使霍尔木兹海峡明天重新开放，供应链和通胀的恢复速度也可能远低于市场预期。这是针对仅凭停火公告就预期能源和商品成本将迅速回归正常这一观点发出的尖锐警告。他指出，美国货币政策仍具有一定紧缩性，而霍尔木兹海峡的重新开放是决定未来走向的关键变量。日本央行政策委员会成员小枝顺子指出，石油对日本而言构成负面供给冲击，这与日本央行内部对这场冲突经济影响的评估框架一致。 技术与市场 美国商务部周日堵上了一个漏洞，此前近一年来，中国企业曾通过位于马来西亚等地的海外子公司，在未获许可的情况下收购英伟达Blackwell、Rubin和AMD MI350x芯片。一位业内人士估计，可能已有数十万枚芯片通过这一漏洞被出口，这可能削弱了华盛顿用于遏制中国人工智能发展的核心手段之一。该指导方针并未要求关闭现有设施。伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司同意以85亿美元现金收购住宅建筑商泰勒·莫里森，较上周五收盘价溢价24%，该交易预计将于今年下半年完成。受战争风险影响，美元汇率小幅走高。区域股市延续了创纪录的涨势。美国股指期货开盘走软，随后转为上涨。特朗普的军事行动现已进入第四个月。本文由investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹撰写。
items.summaryEn: Reports of another likely ballistic missile interception near Ali Al Salem Airbase, Kuwait Japan Q1 capex growth stalls at near zero, raising risk of GDP revision lower ECB's Schnabel says Iran war inflation too broad to look through, flags rate hikes China tightens outbound investment rules to curb technology and data transfers Rubio pushes Lebanon ceasefire but US says Hezbollah blocked deal on Iran's orders Is Palantir a Buy? China May private survey Rating Dog Manufacturing PMI 51.8 (expected 51.4, prior 52.2) Reports of Iran drafting its own deal amendments as Tehran pushes back on Trump's terms PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8167 (vs. estimate at 6.7643Z) Nasdaq technical analysis today at investingLive.com Japan manufacturing PMI eases to 54.5 in May. Cost pressures hit 32-month high Powell warns Fed independence at risk if officials can be removed over policy Bitcoin Futures Analysis Today: BTC Stalls Below 74,535 Japan Capital Spending Q1: 0.0% y/y (expected 4.0%, prior 6.5%) UK businesses remain deeply downbeat on outlook despite slight mood lift in May Australia manufacturing PMI slips to 50.7 in May as new orders hit seven-month low Iran is calling the resignation report fake news. Iran's president resigns, says IRGC seized control and cut him out of decisions US Globex open for Sunday evening trade and the new week. Oil up, Stocks little change. Berkshire Hathaway to buy Taylor Morrison in $8.5bn all-cash housing deal US military guiding ships through Strait of Hormuz. NY Times says 70 in last 3 weeks. US Commerce Department moves to block Nvidia and AMD chip flows to Chinese overseas units Iran says no nuclear commitments made as talks with US continue China manufacturing PMI falls to 50 in May as export orders contract Monday open FX. Indicative rates 01 June 2026 Should you sell DELL stock or move your stop higher? Practical lesson in trade management Week in Focus: 1st - 5th June: Highlights include US ISMs, NFP, EZ HICP & CAD jobs US Bessent on Iran blockade says anything that is taken off will be taken off slowly At a glance: Oil opens higher as US-Iran deal remains elusive; Trump says he is in no hurry, warns military action could resume if talks collapse Iran's President Pezeshkian reportedly asked to resign, citing IRGC takeover of decision-making; Iranian state media and government deny the report Rubio spoke with Lebanese President Aoun and Israeli PM Netanyahu on ceasefire initiative; US official warns failure could give Israel green light to resume Beirut strikes Missile and drone attacks reported near Ali Al Salem Airbase in Kuwait; Kuwaiti air defences engaged US Navy quietly escorted around 70 vessels through Hormuz over three weeks, averaging three per day against a pre-war baseline of 100-plus daily China RatingDog/S&P Global Manufacturing PMI eases to 51.8 in May from 52.2; input inflation slows for first time in six months but remains above long-run average ECB's Schnabel signals rate hikes ahead, says Iran war inflation too broad to look through Fed's Kashkari warns supply chain and inflation recovery could lag even after Hormuz reopens; BOJ's Koeda flags oil as negative supply shock for Japan US closes AI chip export loophole; hundreds of thousands of advanced Nvidia and AMD chips may have reached Chinese firms via overseas subsidiaries over the past year Berkshire Hathaway agrees to acquire Taylor Morrison for $8.5bn, a 24% premium to Friday's close USD edges higher; regional equities extend record run; US futures open soft before turning positive Oil prices climbed at the open and regional sentiment stayed cautious as the Iran-US nuclear framework showed fresh signs of strain, a reported presidential resignation in Tehran rattled confidence in the diplomatic track, and Washington moved to close a year-long loophole that had been quietly feeding China's AI ambitions. Iran: the deal and the denial The session's dominant story was a report, first published by Iran International, that President Masoud Pezeshkian had submitted a resignation to the Supreme Leader's office, citing his exclusion from major decisions and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' effective takeover of government. According to the report, Pezeshkian told Khamenei directly that he and his administration had been sidelined, leaving him unable to fulfil his responsibilities as president. Iran's Government Information Council pushed back hard, describing the story as foreign propaganda designed to sow discord and insisting Pezeshkian was actively running the country. Iranian state media also denied the report. The denial did not land cleanly. The specific detail in the original account, that the IRGC had assumed control of all major decision-making and that the elected presidency had been rendered ceremonial, was too precise and too politically consequential to be dismissed as routine disinformation, and markets treated it accordingly. The timing is acutely damaging for the negotiations. Washington has been engaging with Iran's civilian foreign ministry on a framework that would extend the ceasefire, begin reopening the Strait of Hormuz and launch structured nuclear talks. If Pezeshkian's account is accurate, the body the US has been negotiating with has been operating without a mandate from the institution that controls Iran's missiles, its naval forces in the strait, and its regional proxies. That is not a technicality. It is a fundamental question about whether any agreement reached with Iranian civilian officials can be implemented or honoured. Trump, speaking to Fox News on Saturday, said he was in no hurry to reach a deal, adding that a rushed negotiation would produce a bad outcome. He stressed he was pressing for a framework that would permanently prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon and warned that if talks collapsed, military action could resume. Separately, a US official told Axios that the failure of Secretary Rubio's Lebanon ceasefire push, which sought a Hezbollah halt to attacks as a first step toward de-escalation, could lead Washington to give Israel a green light to resume strikes on Beirut targets. Reports of missile and drone attacks near Ali Al Salem Airbase in Kuwait added to the session's geopolitical noise. Shipping: a trickle, not a reopening The New York Times reported that US Central Command had quietly coordinated passage for around 70 commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz over three weeks, with ships running dark to avoid Iranian detection. The figure is higher than some analysts had expected but needs context: before the Trump war began, more than 100 vessels transited the strait daily. Three coordinated crossings per day is a managed trickle, not a restoration of normal traffic, and the covert nature of the operation means independent verification is impossible. Central banks ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel struck a clearly hawkish note, saying the bank can no longer look through the Iran war's inflationary impact now that price pressures have spread beyond energy and the risk of unanchored expectations has risen. She said infrastructure damage and supply chain disruption have altered price dynamics in a lasting way and declined to put a ceiling on the number of rate hikes required, saying the ECB would continue assessing data and regional developments. Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari offered a sobering counterpoint on the relief-rally thesis. Even if the Strait of Hormuz were to reopen tomorrow, he said, the speed at which supply chains and inflation recover could be considerably slower than markets might expect. It was a pointed warning against pricing in a rapid normalisation of energy and goods costs simply on the back of a ceasefire announcement. US monetary policy remains somewhat restrictive, he noted, and the Hormuz reopening is the key variable determining the path forward. Bank of Japan Policy Board member Junko Koeda noted that oil represents a negative supply shock for Japan, consistent with the BOJ's internal framing of the conflict's economic impact. Technology and markets The Commerce Department closed a loophole on Sunday that had for nearly a year allowed Chinese firms to acquire Nvidia Blackwell, Rubin and AMD MI350x chips through overseas subsidiaries, primarily in locations such as Malaysia, without a licence. One industry source estimated that hundreds of thousands of chips may have been exported through the gap, potentially undermining one of Washington's central tools for slowing China's AI development. The guidance does not require existing installations to be shut down. Berkshire Hathaway agreed to acquire homebuilder Taylor Morrison for $8.5 billion in cash, at a 24% premium to Friday's close, in a deal expected to close in the second half of the year. The US dollar edged higher on war risk. Regional equities extended their record run. US equity index futures opened soft before turning positive. Trump's excursion is now a war in its fourth month. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap: Trump touts naval blockade lift, progress toward deal
items.summary: Trump did not reach a decision on any new deal with Iran after Situation Room meeting How have interest rate expectations changed after this week's event? Iran's Baghaei: No agreement has been finalised with the United States so far Fed's Daly says she doesn't see mass unemployment or displacement from AI Blockade lift announcement by Trump seen as first step toward broader US-Iran agreement Iran's Fars News says Iranian sources deny Trump's latest comments Trump: The US naval blockade will now be lifted Tech sector surges while communication services face downturn Trump administration wants autos under USMCA to be at least 50% made in the US - WSJ Major US stock indices extend gains to new record highs on US-Iran deal optimism Fed's Paulson says healthy for markets to shift to tighter monetary policy outlook Iran's top negotiator Ghalibaf: We gain concessions not with talks, but with missiles Fed's Bowman says progress on lowering inflation has stalled US April wholesale inventories +0.5% vs +0.8% expected US April advanced goods trade balance -$82.40 billion vs -$86.50 billion expected Canada Q1 GDP -0.1% vs +1.5% expected Germany May preliminary CPI +2.6% vs +2.9% y/y expected The highlight of the session was Trump's announcement on Truth Social of the lifting of the US naval blockade and a "final determination" on a broader agreement to follow shortly in the White House Situation Room. This gave the positive risk sentiment a further boost, with oil prices extending losses. Iranian sources suggested the public blockade announcement was viewed as the first step in a wider framework that could eventually address regional security issues, sanctions, and future discussions on Iran’s nuclear program. Iranian sources pushed back on Trump’s characterization of the talks though. Sources cited by Iran’s Fars News Agency described Trump’s statements as a mixture of “truth and falsehood,” denied claims regarding the dismantling of Iran’s nuclear material, and insisted that no final agreement has been approved. Tehran reportedly maintains that discussions remain focused on ending the conflict and lifting the blockade, while key issues such as frozen Iranian assets, sanctions relief, and broader political conditions remain unresolved. Later reports indicated that Trump’s nearly two-hour Situation Room meeting ended without a final decision on a new agreement. According to US officials, the administration believes it is close to a deal, but significant sticking points remain, particularly regarding the unfreezing of Iranian funds and the sequencing of commitments by both sides. As a result, while markets interpreted the developments as a sign of diplomatic progress, negotiations continue and a formal agreement has yet to be finalized by either Washington or Tehran. Despite the noise, the constant push towards a deal continues to keep the markets supported. Federal Reserve officials collectively reinforced a cautious and generally neutral policy stance, emphasizing that inflation remains above target and that current interest-rate setting remains appropriate. Fed's Bowman warned that progress on disinflation has stalled and said an extended Middle East-driven energy shock could add to inflation pressures later this year, though she argued against overreacting to temporary price spikes. Fed's Paulson similarly stated that inflation was too high even before the recent geopolitical tensions, supported holding rates at mildly restrictive levels, and said it was healthy for markets to shift toward a tighter-for-longer policy outlook. Meanwhile, Fed's Daly expressed confidence that monetary policy is well positioned to restore price stability without unnecessarily harming the economy, while highlighting the potential for AI-driven productivity gains to support growth and ease long-run inflationary pressures. On the economic data side, Germany’s headline CPI slowed to 2.6% on easing energy prices, but core inflation rose to 2.5% vs 2.3% in the prior month. Canada’s GDP grew just 0.1% in the first quarter, well below the 1.5% consensus forecast. The Canadian data has been consistently surprising to the downside lately which makes the market's BoC rate hike expectations look off. In the US, trade and inventory data were more encouraging. The April advanced goods trade deficit narrowed to $82.4 billion from the expected $86.5 billion, suggesting net exports could provide a more favorable contribution to second-quarter GDP than previously anticipated. Meanwhile, wholesale inventories rose 0.5%, below the 0.8% forecast. Have a great weekend! This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 特朗普在情况室会议后未就与伊朗达成任何新协议作出决定 本周事件后，市场对利率的预期发生了哪些变化？ 伊朗巴盖伊：迄今尚未与美国敲定任何协议 美联储的戴利表示，她不认为人工智能会导致大规模失业或人员替代 特朗普宣布解除封锁被视为迈向更广泛美伊协议的第一步 伊朗法尔斯通讯社称，伊朗消息人士否认特朗普的最新言论 特朗普： 美国海军封锁现已解除 科技板块大涨，通信服务板块面临下行 据《华尔街日报》报道，特朗普政府希望《美墨加协定》下的汽车至少50%在美国制造 受美伊协议乐观情绪提振，美国主要股指延续涨势创下新高 美联储的保尔森表示，市场转向更紧缩的货币政策预期对市场有利 伊朗首席谈判代表加利巴夫： 我们获得的让步不是靠谈判，而是靠导弹 美联储的鲍曼表示，降低通胀的进展已停滞 美国4月批发库存环比增长0.5%，低于预期0.8% 美国4月商品贸易顺差为-824亿美元，好于预期-865亿美元 加拿大第一季度GDP环比下降0.1% （预期为+1.5%） 德国5月初步CPI同比+2.6%（预期为+2.9%） T 本次会议的亮点是特朗普在“真相社交”平台上宣布解除美国对伊朗的海上封锁，并表示将在白宫情况室很快就一项更广泛的协议作出“最终决定”。这进一步提振了市场风险偏好，油价延续跌势。伊朗消息人士表示，此次公开宣布解除封锁被视为更广泛框架下的第一步，该框架最终可能解决地区安全问题、制裁问题以及未来关于伊朗核计划的讨论。不过，伊朗消息人士对特朗普对会谈的描述提出了异议。伊朗法尔斯通讯社援引的消息人士称，特朗普的言论是“真假参半”，否认了关于拆除伊朗核材料的说法，并坚称尚未批准任何最终协议。据报道，德黑兰方面坚持认为，讨论仍集中在结束冲突和解除封锁上，而伊朗被冻结资产、制裁缓解以及更广泛的政治条件等关键问题仍未解决。后续报道显示，特朗普在情况室近两小时的会议结束后，并未就新协议作出最终决定。 据美国官员称，美国政府认为已接近达成协议，但仍存在重大分歧，特别是关于解冻伊朗资金以及双方履行承诺的顺序问题。因此，尽管市场将这些进展解读为外交取得进展的信号，但谈判仍在继续，华盛顿和德黑兰双方均尚未敲定正式协议。尽管外界议论纷纷，但各方持续推动达成协议的努力仍在支撑着市场。美联储官员们一致重申了审慎且总体中性的政策立场，强调通胀仍高于目标水平，当前的利率设定依然恰当。美联储委员鲍曼警告称，通胀缓解的进程已陷入停滞，并表示由中东局势引发的能源冲击若持续，可能在今年晚些时候加剧通胀压力，不过她反对对暂时性的价格飙升反应过度。美联储委员保尔森同样指出，即使在近期地缘政治紧张局势出现之前，通胀水平也已过高，他支持将利率维持在温和紧缩的水平，并表示市场转向“更长时间保持紧缩”的政策预期是健康的。 与此同时，美联储理事戴利表示，对货币政策能够在不给经济造成不必要损害的情况下恢复物价稳定充满信心，同时强调了人工智能驱动的生产率提升有望支撑经济增长并缓解长期通胀压力。在经济数据方面，受能源价格回落影响，德国整体CPI增速放缓至2.6%，但核心通胀率从上月的2.3%升至2.5%。加拿大第一季度GDP仅增长0.1%，远低于1.5%的共识预期。近期加拿大经济数据持续低于预期，这使得市场对加拿大央行加息的预期显得有些失准。在美国，贸易和库存数据则更为乐观。4月商品贸易赤字从预期的865亿美元收窄至824亿美元，这表明净出口对第二季度GDP的贡献可能比此前预期的更为积极。与此同时，批发库存增长0.5%，低于0.8%的预期。祝大家周末愉快！本文由investinglive.com的Giuseppe Dellamotta撰写。
